legal_entities_covered: Fresenius Kabi Limited and Calea UK Limited (together Fresenius Kabi UK), both subsidiaries of FHC (Holdings) Limited; parent: Fresenius SE & Co KGaA
document_title: Fresenius Kabi Limited UK Carbon Reduction Plan
scope_geography: UK-specific (two UK sites in Runcorn: Cestrian Court 1 and 2)
usable_as_direct_label: Yes - UK-specific figures for real UK operational entity with physical sites; best pharma/healthcare supplier CRP found so far
verification_level: Not explicitly stated as third-party verified; ISO 14001 certified; EcoVadis assessed globally; CDP reporter via parent Fresenius SE
notes: BEST PHARMA ENTRY SO FAR: Fresenius Kabi UK is a genuine UK pharmaceutical/healthcare supplier with physical manufacturing/compounding/warehouse sites in Runcorn. Scope 3 jumped from 5780 (2021 baseline) to 58206 tCO2e (2024) - a 10x increase explained by completing a FULL Scope 3 analysis in 2024 for ALL relevant categories (1,2,3,4,5,6,7,11,12) vs only categories 4,5,6,7 in baseline year. This is NOT an emissions increase - it is a methodology expansion. Critical for your model: DO NOT interpret the 2021->2024 Scope 3 trend as real emissions change; only the S1+S2 trend is comparable across years. Cat1 (Purchased Goods & Services) included in 2024 using spend-based screening method. Revenue not disclosed but Fresenius Kabi UK is a large SECR-qualifying entity. Scope 3 Cat 11 (Use of sold products) included - unusual and potentially valuable for understanding downstream carbon of medical devices/pharma sold to NHS.

notes: UK-specific figures confirmed for DSC UK entity. However DHL is NHS logistics/distribution partner (Scope 3 Cat 4 from NHS perspective) not a product manufacturer - so this CRP is directly relevant for NHS UPSTREAM TRANSPORT CARBON (Cat 4 in your DEFRA mapping) rather than pharmaceutical manufacturing carbon. Scope 1 of 134918 tCO2e reflects large UK distribution fleet. Figures reported Well-to-Wheel (WtW) which includes upstream fuel production - important methodological note when comparing to other suppliers who may use Tank-to-Wheel. Strong verification credentials. Achieved 32% reduction vs 2021 baseline. Note DHL revised baseline from 2019 to 2021 following SBTi restatement - previous 2019 baseline in earlier CRP versions should not be compared directly.

notes: Searched reckitt.com, NHS Evergreen portal, and general web search - no PPN 06/21 Carbon Reduction Plan found for any UK Reckitt entity. Reckitt publishes comprehensive global sustainability reporting (2025 Sustainability Report, ESG Data Book, Basis of Reporting) but these are corporate-level global documents not PPN 06/21 filings. Reckitt healthcare division (Strepsils, Gaviscon, Dettol, Durex) is a significant NHS-relevant supplier. Their 2019 climate change insight document and 2025 sustainability report both confirmed as wrong document type - no SECR declaration, no PPN format. Possible explanations: (1) Reckitt files CRP under a specific subsidiary name not easily searchable, (2) they use the parent PPN 06/21 exemption route, or (3) their CRP is not publicly accessible. This absence itself is a citable data point: a major NHS supplier with no publicly locatable PPN 06/21 CRP. Record as gap in dataset with global report as context only.
